notes:
DSV4 default-system-prompt injection — partial mitigation for multi-turn chat issues.

Fix: When family_name=='deepseek_v4' and no system message provided, inject 'You are a helpful AI assistant. Respond directly and concisely.' Applied at all 3 server.py sites: /v1/chat/completions, /api/chat (Ollama), /v1/responses. Each logs INFO line: 'DSV4: injected default system prompt'.

Per ml-explore/mlx-lm PR #1195 testing instructions, DSV4 requires a system prompt to anchor multi-turn chat — without one the model goes into reasoning-runaway (529+ token reasoning blocks never closed with </think>) and on interruption leaves bad cache state that destabilizes subsequent turns.

HONEST DISCLOSURE — PARTIAL MITIGATION ONLY:

The DSV4-Flash-JANGTQ (2-bit) bundle has inherent quality issues that the engine cannot fully fix:

1. 2-bit codebook precision floor — HumanEval pass@1=26% on JANGTQ vs 67% on JANGTQ4. Use DeepSeekV4-Flash-JANGTQ4 (4-bit) for chat reliability.

2. Numerical drift R1+R2 — mHC pre-mix RMS in bf16 over 16,384-element vector + MLA absorb-form SDPA in bf16 over 512-dim contraction accumulate noise exceeding signal at ~2k-4k decode steps. Model fails to emit </think> reliably, causing reasoning runaway.

3. Training-data contamination in chat-mode (already mitigated in 1.3.96 via auto-flip to thinking-mode).

RECOMMENDATION: Use JANGQ-AI/DeepSeekV4-Flash-JANGTQ4 (4-bit codebook) instead of JANGTQ (2-bit) for chat workloads. The 2-bit JANGTQ remains useful for memory-constrained edge deployments. Lower temperature (0.3-0.6) helps; high temp (1.0) amplifies numerical noise.

Update channels: GH release v1.3.97, latest.json 1.3.97, PyPI vmlx 1.3.97, engine __version__ 1.3.97, panel package.json 1.3.97.
